title: Evangelion 333 N3I Moon Turning Into Core
display_title: Evangelion 333 N3I Moon Turning Into Core
wikitext:
[]: Evangelion 3.33 N3I Moon Turning Into Core?             Quote Post by shinhito » Sun Apr 26, 2015 4:50 am I was just rewatching Evangelion 3.33 again, and I noticed something during the Aftermath of Near 3rd Impact reveal sequence. The texture of the red grid on the moon looked suspiciously like the core "pattern" that is used throughout the NTE series. Please look at the full resolution image, you can see visible spots that are similar to the Shito cores in the NTE movies. The Seele Monoliths also have a similar pattern in 3.33 but are still distinct from the more simplistic spotted red core pattern. SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sun Apr 26, 2015 1:53 pm Ooh, pretty interesting. Could have some correlation, with seemingly everything else turning into core.

[]:             Quote Post by kuribo-04 » Mon Apr 27, 2015 12:39 pm My theory is that this time the point of the Impacts is to turn the whole planet into one core, includiing the creatures on it (Instrumentality). That's why the Earth has a mouth in 1.0. The Awakened Evas do have the powers to turn stuff into core, so it seems that's the objective. Also, I seem to remember that designs of 1.0 mention that Anno wanted to give the core's more important. That's wht they have this weird CG effect. Anno wants it to look like "there is a world in there". I don't know if the Moon will be included in this process, but since it's nearing Earth, why not?
